title: PersonaSpec
description: Reference-only local snapshot of the opifex PersonaSpec contract. This file is non-authoritative for canonical admission semantics; larva.core.validate enforces the canonical boundary.

id.description: Stable canonical persona identity. Required by schema (missing id is invalid). Flat kebab-case, no namespaces in v1. Used by registry, resolution, promotion, and dispatch references.

description.description: Short operational summary of the persona's purpose and capability boundary. For humans and orchestrators, not a second prompt.

prompt.description: Fully composed executable prompt text. This is the final baked artifact, not a template. Assembly may exist upstream but canonical PersonaSpec stores the result.

model.description: Default model identifier, e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514'. Model selection is part of persona execution identity.

tools.description: DEPRECATED: canonical admission rejects this field. Present only as a local compatibility annotation in a non-authoritative snapshot schema.

capabilities.description: Map of tool_family -> posture. The canonical tool-access declaration surface per ADR-002.

model_params.description: Provider-specific LLM inference parameters. additionalProperties allows provider-specific params (e.g. top_k, frequency_penalty).

side_effect_policy.description: DEPRECATED per ADR-002: canonical admission rejects this field. Runtime policy ownership moved to anima; this local schema is non-authoritative.

can_spawn.description: Spawn capability boundary. false/omitted = no spawn. true = spawn allowed, targets are a runtime concern. string[] = spawn restricted to listed canonical persona ids.

compaction_prompt.description: Persona-specific prompt for context compaction/summarization. Optional but changes execution behavior.

spec_version.description: Schema version envelope (semver). All consumers must reject unknown versions.

spec_digest.description: SHA-256 of canonical JSON (sorted keys, no whitespace, spec_digest excluded from input). Computed by larva; optional in raw input, always present in larva output.
